name: Anandi Woodworking Lab - For Ages 6 - 13
description: Anandi School invites you to join the  Anandi   Woodworking Lab  In this hands-on woodworking workshop, children will design and build a floating jar décor piece from scratch. Using real tools and materials, they will learn fundamental woodworking skills while creating something functional, beautiful, and uniquely their own. The Build A handcrafted wooden frame that suspends a glass jar using rope - perfect as a planter, flower holder, desk accessory, or decorative piece for their room. Children can personalise their creation through design choices, decorative details, and finishing touches. What They'll Learn Safe handling of woodworking tools Measuring, marking, and planning a build Basic techniques for cutting, assembling, and finishing wood How balance, structure, and design work together Creative problem-solving through hands-on making What They'll Do Measure and mark wood with accuracy Learn safe sawing and cutting techniques Hammer, nail, and assemble components Sand and polish their finished project Create a rope suspension system for the jar Add personal design details and finishing touches Age Group: 7–13 years By the end of the workshop, participants will take home a handcrafted décor piece, practical woodworking skills, and the satisfaction of creating something with their own hands. Limited spots available. ABOUT ANANDI SCHOOL, SARJAPUR ROAD Anandi School is a future-ready IB school where students learn by doing. It’s the only school with the Harvard-inspired 4+1 Field Model, where four days are dedicated to academic learning and one day each week is dedicated to solving real-world challenges alongside mentors including entrepreneurs, scientists, artists, and changemakers. Connect here to register:   aishwaryas@anandi.org The roads leading up to Sabha are undergoing maintenance, please plan to arrive early and avail of public transport. Limited parking is available at the venue only for individuals with mobility challenges.
